ops.title: daemonkit decision: version taxonomy + dev-wins polarity
ops.body:
**Decision (hardening pass, 2026-07-18):** the donors genuinely conflict — cc-interact ranks any non-triple string as dev; cc-orchestrate emits `9999.<mtime-ns>.0-dev` which PARSES as a release triple, so under a naive `Newer` a plain `"dev"` outranks the mtime form, inverting newest-dev-wins.

**daemonkit `version` contract:** `Parse` classifies `Release{Major,Minor,Patch}` vs `Dev{BuildUnixNano}` BEFORE triple parsing (the `9999.` sentinel and no-triple strings both classify Dev). `DevString` is the one canonical dev form. Every Dev outranks every Release; Dev-vs-Dev orders by nanoseconds. Identity is resolved and stat'd exactly once at startup and memoized — a rebuilt binary must not change a running daemon's reported version. Required table test: release / legacy plain-dev / canonical mtime-dev / two same-second rebuilds.

**fusekit self-retire flips polarity** to dev-wins — a dev-only behavioral delta, isolated commit, flagged in review.

**Guard:** dev daemons never own production endpoints — dev/test builds use a separate socket namespace (or parent-bound lifetime) with teardown verification; a leaked Dev daemon outranking every release on the production socket is the failure mode dev-wins would otherwise create.
